name: Electrocution · electrical burn
summary: Electric shock from mains or equipment. Can stop the heart. Internal burns may not be visible.

callEmergency.text: Call 999 — always seek medical advice for electrical burns, even small ones.

steps: FIRST: cut the power at the mains. Don't touch the person until you're certain the electricity is off — you'll be electrocuted too.
Once safe, assess: are they conscious and breathing?
If conscious — comfort and reassure. Seek medical advice for any burn.
If unconscious and not breathing normally — call 999, start CPR.
Cool any visible burn under running water for 20 minutes (see Severe burns).

warnings.text: NEVER touch the casualty until the power supply is confirmed off.
Internal burns may track along the path of the current and not be visible. Always seek medical advice.
